$schema: Ombuto Code Tools Manifest — describes available CLI tools for AI coding agents

tools.name: db-query
tools.description: Query the Ombuto Code SQLite database. Read-only — no modifications allowed. Use this instead of Python/sqlite3.

commands.name: tables
commands.description: List all tables in the database

commands.name: schema
commands.description: Show the CREATE TABLE statement for a specific table

commands.name: query
commands.description: Run an arbitrary read-only SELECT query against the database
commands.input: SQL SELECT statement (string, must start with SELECT)
commands.output: Formatted table with column headers and rows
commands.notes: Only SELECT queries are allowed. The database uses json_extract() for accessing ticket fields stored as JSON in the 'data' column.
examples: node .ombutocode/tools/db-query.js query "SELECT id, json_extract(data, '$.title') as title FROM backlog_tickets LIMIT 10"

node .ombutocode/tools/db-query.js query "SELECT id, json_extract(data, '$.status') as status, json_extract(data, '$.epic_ref') as epic FROM backlog_tickets WHERE json_extract(data, '$.status') = 'todo'"

commands.name: tickets
commands.description: List all backlog tickets with key fields. Optionally filter by status.
commands.input: --status (optional) — filter by ticket status (e.g. backlog, todo, in_progress, review, done)
commands.output: Formatted table: id | title | status | assignee | epic_ref

commands.name: ticket
commands.description: Show full JSON details of a single ticket by ID
commands.output: Full ticket JSON including all fields: title, status, assignee, epic_ref, references, acceptance_criteria, dependencies, notes

commands.name: epics
commands.description: List all epic files from docs/Epics/ with their title and status (reads from filesystem, not database)

commands.name: stats
commands.description: Show ticket counts grouped by status

database_schema_notes.backlog_tickets: Each row has 'id' (TEXT PK), 'sort_order' (INTEGER), and 'data' (TEXT — JSON blob). Use json_extract(data, '$.field') to access fields.
common_fields_in_data: $.dependencies — JSON array of ticket ID strings
$.references — optional object with prd, architecture, style_guide, mockups paths

tools.name: ticket-write
tools.description: Insert new tickets into the ombutocode backlog database from a JSON array file. This is the canonical way to write tickets — do NOT hand-roll sql.js scripts. Use db-query for reads and this tool for writes.

commands.name: insert
commands.description: Insert tickets from a JSON array file. Validates required fields, checks for id collisions, appends after MAX(sort_order), bumps the 'backlog:updated_at' metadata key, and creates a pre-insert database backup by default.
commands.input: Path to a JSON file containing an array of ticket objects. Each ticket must have at minimum: id, title, status, epic_ref. Other common fields: assignee, acceptance_criteria, dependencies, references, notes, description, last_updated.
commands.output: Summary of planned inserts and, on success, list of inserted ticket ids and verification hint.

flags.description: Validate the input and print planned inserts without writing to the database
Skip creating a pre-insert database backup
Overwrite ticket ids that already exist (INSERT OR REPLACE)

notes: The id field is stored in its own column and stripped from the JSON blob written to backlog_tickets.data.
By default the tool creates a backup at ombutocode.db.before-insert-<timestamp> and leaves it in place; remove it after verifying the inserts.
Verify inserts with: node .ombutocode/tools/db-query.js tickets --status backlog
All inserts run inside a single transaction — either every ticket in the batch is written, or none are.

example.title: Create users and sessions database tables

tools.name: svg-to-png
tools.description: Convert SVG files to PNG images. Use this for generating UI mockups: write SVG markup (text-based, agents can do this reliably), then convert to PNG. No Python or native build tools required.
tools.workflow: 1. Write an SVG file to docs/Mockups/Name.svg  2. Convert to PNG: node .ombutocode/tools/svg-to-png.js docs/Mockups/Name.svg  3. PNG is saved alongside the SVG

options.description: Output width in pixels (default: from SVG or 1200)
Output height in pixels (default: from SVG or 800)
Scale factor for higher resolution (e.g. 2 for retina)

notes.svg_tips: Write standard SVG with inline styles. Use viewBox for dimensions. Use web-safe fonts (Segoe UI, system-ui, sans-serif). The SVG source is kept alongside the PNG for future editing.
notes.why_svg_first: Agents can reliably write SVG (it's text/XML). Python may not be available on all machines. This tool bridges SVG to PNG using the sharp package (prebuilt binaries, no build tools needed).
notes.accepted_input: Any valid SVG file. Can include embedded images, gradients, filters, text, shapes.
notes.output: PNG file with transparency support. Reports dimensions and file size on success.

usage_notes.for_agents: Before using Python, PIL, sqlite3, or any external tool, check this manifest for Node.js alternatives that are guaranteed to work. If Python is not available, use these Node.js tools as a fallback.
usage_notes.tool_discovery: Read .ombutocode/tools/tools.json to discover all available tools, their commands, and usage examples.
usage_notes.mockup_workflow: For UI mockups: write SVG file → convert with svg-to-png tool → PNG saved to docs/Mockups/
